title: Stellantis TIPM Fuel Pump Relay Failure (2007-2016 Jeep/Chrysler/Dodge)
description: The TIPM (Totally Integrated Power Module) internal fuel pump relay failure is the most common Jeep/Chrysler no-start and intermittent stall complaint. Covers diagnosis, bypass wire test, and TIPM replacement vs relay bypass fix.
common_symptoms: engine stalls randomly and won't restart for 15-30 minutes
hear starter but no fuel pump prime
fuel pump runs continuously after engine off
horn honking randomly or windows rolling down by themselves (TIPM controls everything)
physical_solution: Diagnose by listening for fuel pump prime on key-on. If no prime sound: bypass test at TIPM fuel pump relay socket. Confirm TIPM failure. Fix: TIPM replacement (expensive) or fuel pump relay external bypass kit (cheap, effective).

uds_services: $19 02 FF (read DTCs from BCM/TIPM if accessible)
$22 ReadDataByIdentifier (fuel pump relay status if available)
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — TIPM modules are on CAN-IHS (pins 3/11) — not accessible via standard ELM327. Diagnosis is primarily physical (listen for pump prime). Powertrain reads via standard ELM327 still work. P0230 (fuel pump circuit) readable via Mode $03.
preconditions: Verify: listen for fuel pump prime buzz (2-second hum from fuel tank) on key-on
Check: battery voltage (TIPM issues worsen with low voltage)

procedure_steps.description: LISTEN TEST: Turn key to ON (not start). Listen at fuel tank area for 2-second electric hum (fuel pump prime). If no hum = no fuel pump activation. Repeat 3x — if intermittent pump prime = classic TIPM relay failure.
Read P0230 (fuel pump primary circuit): Mode $03 or $19 to PCM. P0230 stored during no-start event confirms fuel pump circuit failure detected by ECU. P0230 intermittent = TIPM relay sticking.

BYPASS TEST: Locate TIPM under hood (right side fender, black module). Find C3 connector and fuel pump relay output pin (typically pin 85/87 area depending on model). Jump 12V directly to fuel pump feed wire. If pump primes and engine starts = TIPM confirmed bad.

TIPM BYPASS KIT (permanent fix option): Install external relay bypass kit (EasyFix or equivalent, ~$20-40). Kit taps battery power and uses a standalone relay triggered by PCM fuel pump command signal. Bypasses TIPM relay entirely. Vehicle functions normally — permanent fix without replacing $1,200+ TIPM.

TIPM REPLACEMENT (full fix): OEM TIPM replacement requires VIN-specific programming. Used TIPM requires wiTECH programming. Cost: $800-$1,500 parts + programming. Not necessary if bypass kit resolves complaint.

OTHER TIPM FAILURES: Same TIPM controls: horn (random honking), windows (random roll-down), wipers (won't turn off), A/C (random activation). If customer reports multiple electrical gremlins = TIPM is the common failure module.

After bypass install or TIPM replacement: verify fuel pump primes on every key-on. Drive cycle 30 minutes. Verify no P0230 return. Verify no other TIPM-controlled systems misbehaving. Test horn, windows, wipers.

oem_notes.Stellantis: TIPM failure affects: 2007-2016 Jeep Grand Cherokee, Wrangler JK, Liberty, Compass, Patriot; 2007-2016 Chrysler 300, Town & Country; 2007-2016 Dodge Journey, Durango, Grand Caravan, Avenger, Charger; 2007-2012 Ram 1500/2500/3500. Class action lawsuits filed. Chrysler extended warranty on TIPM to 10 years/150k miles on some VINs (check NHTSA database by VIN before billing customer). Bypass kit is most cost-effective solution. Remanufactured TIPM available from Dorman (cost ~$400) as OEM alternative.
